reason: Both give the same core recommendation (contractor bridge + migrate to managed k8s, don't rehire a single replacement) and both correctly frame the 40-services/10-engineers ratio as disproportionate. The differentiators: Answer A explicitly addresses long-term staffing by spreading ops load across existing engineers as a shared responsibility (directly avoiding recreating the bus-factor problem) and explicitly flags service consolidation as a longer-term consideration — hitting all four key points cleanly and concisely. Answer B is also solid and adds nice concrete detail (cost figures, avoiding an ECS/Nomad replatform), but it recommends 'hire one platform-minded engineer... to own infra' afterward, which partially re-creates the single-point-of-failure problem the answer itself just warned against, and it never raises service consolidation as a lever. A's structure more tightly matches the key decision points without the internal tension.
